name: Plain Basic Chili
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 pounds round or chuck steak, trimmed, cut into 1-inch pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large Dutch oven or stockpot, heat the vegetable oil over medium-high heat.
2. When the oil is fragrant, add the onions and garlic and sauté until the onions are tender-crisp, reducing the heat if necessary to keep everything from burning.
3. Add the chili powder and stir well. Add the beef and cook until the beef begins to release its juices, forming a paste with the chili powder.
4. Pour in the beef broth. Use your hands to crush the tomatoes roughly, then add the tomatoes and juices to the pot.
5. Cover and simmer until the beef is tender, about 1 hour.
6. Season with oregano and cumin, adding more or less of each as desired. Taste the chili; if it's too mild, add cayenne pepper to taste.
